resources.string: Start by searching for a stop or a line.
No schedule available for this stop
No stops available for this line
No schedule available at this stop
Download data to use the app without a connection
The Pelo Application is published by Dotshell. Dotshell is not affiliated with TCL or SYTRAL. For any questions, please refer to the contact details provided in the Application.\n
These Terms of Use define the conditions of access and use of the Pelo Application (hereinafter "the Application"). Using the Application implies acceptance of these terms.\n
The Application helps the user navigate the TCL network (Lyon and surrounding area) through search and planning tools. Third-party sources are listed in the "Credits" page.\n

The Application may request the following permissions based on technical needs:
• Location: To display nearby stops (processed locally).
• Network/Internet: To retrieve real-time schedules.

Pelo is designed according to the principle of data protection by design.

5.1. Exclusive Local Storage
Your favorite stops and the detailed history of your trips are stored exclusively locally on your device. Dotshell has no access to this information. You can delete this history at any time from the "Privacy" screen.

5.2. Special Case of Favorites (Socket Connection)
To allow real-time updates of your favorite lines and stops, a temporary technical connection (socket) is established when the application is opened and closed when it is closed.
• This exchange is strictly anonymous.
• No data allowing you to be identified is transmitted or saved on an external server.
• This flow is used solely for the technical synchronization of transport data during your navigation session.

5.3. Data sharing to improve the network
The Application transmits an anonymous message attached to an identifier that changes every 24 hours to Dotshell at each closure. This message contains:
• Your app opening and closing times
• Your stop and line searches
• The itineraries you calculate and choose
• The stops crossed during navigation (never raw GPS coordinates)
• Your clicks on lines and stops
• The official alerts you have read
• The traffic alerts you have reported
• An anonymous usage profile calculated locally (occasional / regular / heavy + up to 5 usual lines)

The daily identifier is randomly generated, contains no link to your phone, and is regenerated each day: it is impossible to track you over time. The transmitted data cannot be retrieved individually and is automatically purged after 90 days.

Your favorite stops, the detailed history of your trips, and the text content of your free entries (addresses) are **never** transmitted. Addresses used in an itinerary calculation are transformed locally into a geographical zone identifier of about 460 m before any transmission.

5.4. No resale to advertisers
Dotshell does not sell your data to advertisers or data brokers. Aggregated and anonymous data may be used or shared with Mobility Organizing Authorities for the purpose of improving the public transport service.

Information (schedules, disruptions) is provided for guidance only. Dotshell declines all responsibility in the event of inaccuracies in data from third parties or unavailability of the transport network.\n
The Application\'s source code is distributed under the GPL-3.0 license. Trademarks and logos of transport networks remain the property of their respective owners.\n
Dotshell reserves the right to modify these Terms. The applicable version is the one available in the Application at the time of its use.\n

The transport data used in the application comes exclusively from the data.grandlyon.com website and is subject to the Mobilités License.

The geographic paths of bus lines (including Chrono, Pleine Lune, Bus relays, Gare Express, Shuttle, Soyeuse, Zone Industrielle, and Junior Direct), tramways, Rhônexpress, Trambus, subways, funiculars, and Navigone are downloaded directly from the public API provided by SYTRAL on the data.grandlyon.com website.

The geographic positions as well as stop names, line contents, and schedules all come from the GTFS (General Transit Feed Specification) file distributed by SYTRAL on the data.grandlyon.com website. This data is manually updated by developers and undergoes pre-processing before use. No data is modified, only its organization is transformed for faster processing by the application.

Line icons also come from data.grandlyon.com and are provided by SYTRAL in SVG format.

Traffic alerts and real-time vehicle positions are provided by SYTRAL on the data.grandlyon.com website via a closed API requiring authentication. Under the Mobilités License, Dotshell makes a mirror of this data publicly available at api.dotshell.eu/pelo/v1/. The mirror makes periodic requests to the SYTRAL API, caches the result, and redistributes a copy to the client, accompanied by the timestamp of the last update. Data is purely copied and redistributed with the exception of traffic alerts, where Junior Direct school buses have been merged with classic lines and duplicates removed.

Map data is provided by MapLibre and OpenStreetMaps.

Map styles (namely Positron, Dark Matter, OSM Bright, and Liberty) are provided by OpenMapTiles. The application uses the Map Tiler Positron (Light) theme by default.

Satellite view tiles come from ESRI World Imagery.

The application is developed independently by Dotshell under the GPL-3.0 license. Pelo is not affiliated with TCL or SYTRAL.

The source code is available on Dotshell's GitHub page at the following repositories: pelo-android for the Android application source code, pelo-ios for the iOS/iPadOS application, raptor-gtfs-pipeline, raptor-kt, and raptor-sw for the RAPTOR routing system, and finally TCL-API-mirror for the real-time data mirror.

Any contribution is open to the community.

For more information, go to dotshell.eu.
Feel free to contact us with any questions, suggestions, or to report a bug.
To report a bug, please send us a message via the contact page of our website [dotshell.eu].
